reasoning: The deployment of conventional, regular US military forces to the terrestrial territory of Yemen is highly improbable. US military operations in Yemen are currently restricted almost exclusively to aerial and naval strikes against Houthi targets. In instances where personnel have operated on the ground historically, they have been Special Operations Forces (SOF), military advisors, or contractors—all of which are explicitly excluded by the resolution criteria. Establishing a conventional ground presence would represent a massive escalation of the Middle East conflict, contravening both the historical aversion of US administrations to ground wars in the region and the likely "America First" posture of the incoming Trump administration, which favors military deterrence and airstrikes over boots on the ground. The only plausible pathways to resolution involve extreme tail risks, such as a major conventional rescue/evacuation operation or a dramatic shift into a broader regional war involving Iran. Thus, the probability is assessed favorably near the market price of ~5.6%.
